content: Evolu mutations (createBadge, completeGoal) are synchronous CRDT operations — no await needed. The async work in useCreateBadge is the signing step (keyProvider.sign); the DB writes are fire-and-forget. Also: credential column uses NonEmptyString (not NonEmptyString1000) so full OB3 JSON of any length is valid.

Uncompressed zlib (DEFLATE stored blocks, BTYPE=00) can generate valid PNG IDAT data without any compression library in React Native. Header: 0x78 0x01, then DEFLATE stored block (BFINAL=1, LEN LE16, NLEN LE16, raw data), then 4-byte Adler-32. The png-chunks-encode library handles PNG-level CRC32. Works for solid-color badge images.

Vendoring for RN platform guards: When openbadges-core has assertBufferAvailable() that throws in React Native, vendor just the baking files (png-baking.ts, png-chunk-utils.ts) into src/badges/, remove the guard, add `import { Buffer } from 'buffer'`. The buffer npm package (v5.7.1) is already a transitive dep and works in Metro/Jest.

const enum with Babel: Babel-based transpilation (babel-preset-expo, babel-jest) does NOT inline const enum values — they become undefined at runtime. Solution: use `const X = { ... } as const; type X = typeof X[keyof typeof X]` pattern instead.

JavaScript bitwise signed overflow: Bitwise ops (<<, |, &, ^) work on signed 32-bit ints. Combining two 16-bit values: `(s2 << 16) | s1` can produce a negative number. Fix: `((s2 << 16) | s1) >>> 0` — unsigned right shift by 0 reinterprets as unsigned 32-bit without changing bits. Required for Adler-32, CRC32, and any checksum/hash calculation.

expo-file-system v19 split: writeAsStringAsync/documentDirectory/EncodingType moved to expo-file-system/legacy. New JSI File/Directory/Paths API is the main export. Binary writes with Uint8Array via File.write() fail at runtime with "unordered_map::at: key not found" when passed a Buffer subclass — use legacy writeAsStringAsync + base64 instead.

OB3 Achievement.image is OPTIONAL — omit it from credentialBuilder.ts rather than embedding a placeholder or local file:// URI. Local file paths are meaningless to external verifiers. The image property should be added only if we have a hosted URI.

A.6 Badge Design System execution order: Wave 1=#178 (types, blocks all). Wave 2=#179 (contours), #180 (dataMapper), #185 (monogram+label) — parallel after #178. Wave 3=#181 (frame interface+boldBorder), #186 (PathText+Banner). Wave 4=#182 (guilloche), #183 (crossHatch+rosette), #184 (microprint+FrameOverlay). Wave 5=#187 (wire renderer). Wave 6=#188 (FrameSelector+CenterMode), #189 (PathTextEditor+BannerEditor). Wave 7=#190 (wire designer). Wave 8=#191 (Storybook+tests). Design doc: docs/plans/2026-02-27-badge-design-system-plan.md

parseBadgeDesign now applies centerMode default ('icon') for legacy JSON. This ensures the type contract (centerMode is required) matches runtime. Pattern: when adding required fields to a type parsed from JSON, apply defaults in the parse function rather than making the field optional.
